all.en: Last winter, I could barely walk through my own living room without bumping into something.
all.zh: 去年冬天，我在自己家客廳走動，幾乎都會撞到東西。

all.en: Boxes from three different moves were still unopened in the corner near the window.
all.zh: 三次搬家累積的紙箱還沒拆，全堆在窗邊的角落。

all.en: One Sunday afternoon, I picked up a book about minimalist living from a friend's shelf.
all.zh: 某個週日下午，我從朋友的書架上拿了一本關於極簡生活的書。

all.en: By the time I finished the introduction, I had already decided to declutter everything.
all.zh: 讀完前言時，我已經決定要把一切都斷捨離。

all.en: I gave myself one month and split the apartment into eight specific zones.
all.zh: 我給自己一個月的時間，並把公寓分成八個明確的區域。

all.en: Each zone got two weekend mornings, plus one weekday evening for finishing touches.
all.zh: 每個區域分配兩個週末早晨，再加一個平日晚上做最後整理。

all.en: The kitchen came first because it was where I spent most of my time daily.
all.zh: 廚房是第一個，因為那是我每天待最久的地方。

all.en: I found nine almost-empty bottles of soy sauce hidden behind cans of cooking oil.
all.zh: 我發現九瓶幾乎空了的醬油，藏在食用油罐的後面。

all.en: There was a rice cooker I had forgotten about, still in its original packaging.
all.zh: 還有一台我完全忘掉的電鍋，連原本的包裝都還沒拆。

all.en: I donated it to a young couple downstairs who had just moved in together.
all.zh: 我把它送給樓下一對剛搬來同住的年輕情侶。

all.en: Their happy faces gave me a small jolt of energy that lasted the rest of the day.
all.zh: 他們開心的臉讓我充飽電，那股動力撐了我一整天。

all.en: The bedroom closet turned out to be the most emotional part of the whole process.
all.zh: 整個過程中，臥室衣櫃竟然是最情緒化的部分。

all.en: I held up a coat I had bought eight years ago for a job interview.
all.zh: 我拿起一件八年前為了面試買的外套。

all.en: I had not worn it once since the day I got the job offer.
all.zh: 從我拿到那份工作的那天起，我就再也沒穿過它。

all.en: It went into the donation pile, along with twenty other things I never used.
all.zh: 它和另外二十件我從沒用過的東西一起進了捐贈堆。

all.en: Letting go felt strange, like I was releasing chapters of my old self into the world.
all.zh: 放手感覺很奇妙，彷彿我把過去自己的章節釋放到世界裡。

all.en: The bathroom was the easiest because most expired products simply went into the trash.
all.zh: 浴室是最容易的，因為大部分過期的產品直接丟進垃圾桶。

all.en: I counted seventeen half-used bottles of shampoo and four identical packs of cotton swabs.
all.zh: 我數了一下，有十七瓶用一半的洗髮精，還有四包一模一樣的棉花棒。

all.en: Books were the hardest because I had collected them since I was a teenager.
all.zh: 書是最難處理的，因為我從青少年時期就開始收藏。

all.en: I kept only the ones I would honestly read again or lend to a close friend.
all.zh: 我只留下那些我真的會再讀一次或借給好朋友的書。

all.en: The rest went to a tiny secondhand shop near the metro station I used.
all.zh: 其餘的都送到了我常用的捷運站附近一家小小的二手書店。

all.en: The owner, an older man named Mr. Lin, smiled and offered me tea every visit.
all.zh: 店主是位姓林的老先生，每次我去他都會微笑著請我喝茶。

all.en: We talked about literature, life, and the joy of letting old stories find new readers.
all.zh: 我們聊文學、聊人生，也聊讓舊故事找到新讀者的喜悅。

all.en: Midway through the project, I hit a wall and wanted to give up entirely.
all.zh: 計畫進行到一半時，我撞牆了，很想完全放棄。

all.en: My sister flew in for the weekend and helped me push through the worst zone.
all.zh: 我姊姊週末飛來幫我，撐過了最難處理的那個區域。

all.en: That zone was the storage room, where I had hidden every problem for years.
all.zh: 那個區域是儲藏室，多年來我把所有問題都藏在那裡。

all.en: We laughed at the cassette tapes, broken hair dryers, and ancient phone chargers.
all.zh: 我們對著錄音帶、壞掉的吹風機和老古董手機充電器笑成一團。

all.en: Three large bags of electronic waste went to the city's recycling center that Monday.
all.zh: 週一那天，三大袋電子廢棄物送到了市區的回收中心。

all.en: By week four, my apartment had become a space where I could actually breathe.
all.zh: 到了第四週，我的公寓變成一個讓我真正能呼吸的空間。

all.en: Every surface had a purpose, and every drawer held only things I genuinely needed.
all.zh: 每個檯面都有它的用途，每個抽屜裡只放真正需要的東西。

all.en: Cleaning now took twenty minutes instead of the dreaded two-hour weekend chore.
all.zh: 現在打掃只要二十分鐘，再也不是讓人害怕的週末兩小時苦差事。

all.en: I started inviting friends over, something I had avoided for nearly five years.
all.zh: 我開始邀請朋友來家裡，這是我近五年來一直在逃避的事。

all.en: The biggest change was not in the apartment but in how my mind felt at home.
all.zh: 最大的改變不是在公寓裡，而是我在家時心情的感受。

all.en: I sleep more deeply now, and my mornings feel lighter and full of fresh air.
all.zh: 我現在睡得更深，早晨感覺輕盈，充滿新鮮空氣。

all.en: Decluttering, it turned out, was never really about the stuff in the first place.
all.zh: 原來，斷捨離從來就不是關於那些東西本身。
